topic: A person finds out they were adopted through a DNA test
raw_output:
[story 1]

The sterile white of the lab room felt suddenly oppressive. Elias stared at the printed report, his breath catching in his throat. Each line of genetic data swam before his eyes, a torrent of unfamiliar markers suddenly connecting him to a family he never knew. His fingers, usually steady when typing code, trembled slightly as he reread the section detailing paternal lineage. A slow, deep thrumming started in his chest, a vibration that seemed to originate from his very core. He pushed back from the desk, the chair scraping loudly against the tile floor. "This can't be right," he murmured, his voice tight. He needed air, needed distance from the cold, undeniable proof laid out before him.

[story 2]

Maya leaned back in her worn armchair, the silence of her apartment amplifying the frantic rhythm of her pulse. The results had landed an hour ago, and she hadn't moved. She picked up her phone, her thumb hovering over the contact list, before dropping it onto the cushion beside her. The sheer weight of the information settled on her shoulders, heavy and suffocating. She closed her eyes, trying to anchor herself against the dizzying sensation that washed over her—a sudden, sharp clarity that made the mundane details of her living room seem painfully vivid. When she opened her eyes, the room looked different; everything felt charged, waiting for a response she couldn't quite articulate.

[story 3]

He paced the length of the living room three times, the hardwood floor protesting beneath his worn slippers. Every movement was jerky, propelled by an internal pressure that demanded release. The DNA confirmation sat on the coffee table, an undeniable artifact of a life he hadn't known existed. A strange stillness settled over him, a kind of hyper-focus where every stray thought, every lingering memory, seemed magnified under a harsh spotlight. He stopped by the window, pressing his forehead against the cool glass, trying to draw in enough oxygen to steady the sudden, overwhelming surge of realization that made his hands clench into fists at his sides.
